claimText: Stem cells are defined by their dual ability to self-renew indefinitely and differentiate into mature, specialized cells.
evidenceExcerpt: Stem cells can self-renew indefinitely and differentiate into mature, specialized cells.
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the fundamental defining characteristics of stem cells.
distractorHooks: Stating that stem cells can differentiate but lose the ability to self-renew
Confusing stem cell self-renewal with the uncontrolled division seen in cancer cells

claimText: Pluripotent stem cells possess the capability to differentiate into all cell types of the adult body.
evidenceExcerpt: Pluripotent stem cells can differentiate into all cells of the adult body.
examUse: Comparison question distinguishing between the differentiation potential of pluripotent versus adult stem cells.
distractorHooks: Stating that pluripotent stem cells are restricted to forming cells of a single specific organ
Confusing pluripotent stem cells with unipotent cells

claimText: Embryonic Stem Cells (ESCs) are naturally found in the early embryo 3 to 5 days after fertilization.
evidenceExcerpt: Embryonic Stem Cells (ESCs) are found in the early embryo 3-5 days post-fertilization.
examUse: Statement-based question on the origin and timeline of Embryonic Stem Cells.
distractorHooks: Claiming ESCs are harvested from the umbilical cord post-delivery
Stating ESCs are found in adult bone marrow

claimText: Induced Pluripotent Stem Cells (iPSCs) are artificially created by introducing embryonic genes into a somatic cell, such as a skin cell, to revert it to a stem cell state.
evidenceExcerpt: Induced Pluripotent Stem Cells (iPSCs) are created by introducing embryonic genes into a somatic cell (e.g., skin cell) to revert it to a stem cell state.
examUse: Statement-based question on the biotechnology process used to generate iPSCs.
distractorHooks: Stating that iPSCs are naturally occurring stem cells found in adult tissues
Claiming iPSCs are created by extracting cells directly from an embryo

claimText: Adult Stem Cells (ASCs) are tissue-specific and generally only yield specialized cells of the specific tissue or organ where they are located.
evidenceExcerpt: Adult Stem Cells (ASCs) are undifferentiated cells in tissues or organs that yield specialized cells of that specific tissue.
examUse: Statement-based question testing the limitations of Adult Stem Cells compared to Pluripotent Stem Cells.
distractorHooks: Asserting that Adult Stem Cells can differentiate into any cell type in the human body
Claiming Adult Stem Cells are a type of Pluripotent stem cell
